title: DFIRCLI Convergence frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a convergence file under convergences/<name>.md. A convergence is a tier-2 multi-artifact join via shared identifier-concept values that yields a higher-level forensic claim.

name.description: Convergence identifier. Must match the filename without the .md extension.

summary.description: One-to-two sentence overview of what this convergence establishes and when it applies.

inputs.description: Input propositions this convergence requires. Each must be a proposition named in some artifact's observations[*].proposition.

input-sources.description: Mapping of which artifacts contribute which input propositions.

join-chain.description: Ordered list of concept-role pivots that thread the input artifacts together. Each step's `concept` must be identifier-kind per the concept registry. May be empty during initial extraction pending authorship.

join-strength.description: Grades how tight this pivot is. strong = globally-unique identifier (UserSID, DeviceSerial, MBRDiskSignature). moderate = session-unique or admin-mutable identifier (LogonSessionId, MachineNetBIOS). weak = heuristic/non-unique correlation (path-match, timestamp-proximity).

description.description: One sentence or short paragraph explaining the forensic role this concept-pivot plays in THIS convergence's inference. Optional but strongly recommended (H-019) — structural enumeration of artifacts alone hides the 'why this join-key matters HERE' layer. Per-concept role descriptions live in concept files (generic); whole-convergence prose lives in notes/summary; this field is the middle layer.

sources.description: Source-registry IDs supporting THIS specific pivot's claim. Strongly recommended — convergence-level `provenance:` applies broadly, but each join-chain entry makes a pivot-specific assertion (e.g. 'MFTEntryReference is globally-unique per NTFS file') that needs its own attribution. Pick 1-3 sources from the convergence's provenance that directly back the pivot's description; do NOT re-enumerate the entire convergence provenance here.

primary-source.description: Single source ID (from schema/sources.yaml) identified during the convergence-pivot audit as the strongest single-source attribution for THIS pivot's claim. Must appear in `sources`. Parallels the exit-node audit field — one canonical citation per claim so an analyst can trace 'why does this join-key belong in this convergence?' to a specific document, not a pile.

attribution-sentence.description: Verbatim sentence extracted from primary-source during the convergence-pivot audit that justifies this pivot in real-world forensic terms. Should make the forensic relevance of the join-key defensible without reading the full source. Parallels the exit-node audit field.

exit-node.description: Terminal node(s) of the join-chain. Must be an identifier-kind concept name OR an artifact flagged `exit-node: true`. May be empty during initial extraction pending authorship. Accepts a single string when one terminus is authoritative, or an array when multiple distinct reasoning paths each close the convergence.

yields.description: What this convergence produces — either a new higher-level proposition, or ceiling elevation on an existing proposition through corroboration.

casey-rationale.description: One-line justification for the ceiling assigned to the new proposition.
One-line justification for WHY the corroborating sources earn the ceiling bump (e.g., 'kernel + EventLog Service are independent subsystems; agreement implies no single-point tamper vector').

via-artifacts.description: DEPRECATED — scheduled for sunset once join-chain is authored for every convergence. Today: legacy/narrative list preserved from the original per-artifact extends-to declarations.

degradation-paths.description: Explicit fallbacks when an input artifact is missing (cleaner ran, acquisition partial).

notes.description: DEPRECATED for new entries — scheduled to fold into `summary:` once each convergence is properly authored. Today: free-form note aggregation from the original per-artifact extends-to text.

provenance.description: Source attribution. Each item is either a source-registry ID (string) or an object {source, section?, note?}. Every ID must resolve to an entry in schema/sources.yaml.
